name: Fillout
what_it_does: Modern form builder with REST API for programmatically reading form submissions, managing forms, and triggering workflows — supporting conditional logic, payments, scheduling, and file uploads.
use_cases: Agents polling or receiving webhook notifications for new form submissions to trigger downstream workflows
Reading and processing survey or intake form responses programmatically for CRM enrichment or data analysis
Creating forms dynamically via API for event registrations, lead capture, or feedback collection campaigns
Integrating form submission data with data pipelines, databases, or ticketing systems without manual export
not_for: Use cases requiring complex multi-page surveys with advanced skip logic that rivals dedicated survey platforms like SurveyMonkey
High-volume data collection exceeding free tier limits without budget for paid plans
Teams needing self-hosted form infrastructure for compliance or data residency requirements
best_when: Best when an agent needs to read and process form submission data in real-time via webhooks or REST polling, especially for teams that want a Typeform-quality builder with a better free tier.
avoid_when: Avoid when you need advanced survey analytics, panel management, or respondent tracking features that go beyond basic form submission collection.

auth.notes: API key passed as a Bearer token in the Authorization header. Generated in the Fillout account settings. Single key per account with no scope granularity — key grants access to all forms and submissions in the account.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Unlimited forms with up to 1,000 submissions per month on the free tier. API access included.
pricing.paid_tiers: Starter plan at $17/month for 5,000 submissions. Pro at $29/month for 25,000 submissions with additional features like file storage and custom domains. Business plan for higher volumes.
pricing.notes: Submission counts reset monthly. Stripe payment processing fees (2.9% + $0.30) are separate from Fillout plan pricing and are charged by Stripe directly.

performance.rate_limits: API rate limits are not explicitly published. Standard fair-use limits apply. Webhook delivery is near-real-time with retry logic on failure.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API returns standard HTTP status codes with JSON error bodies. Error messages are human-readable. Submission API errors (e.g., invalid form ID) are clear and specific.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency keys for API operations. Submission reads are GET operations and naturally safe. Form creation via API (if supported) may create duplicates on retry.
known_agent_gotchas: Form IDs required for all submission queries — must be discovered via form list endpoint or captured from the Fillout dashboard
File upload fields return temporary signed URLs in submission responses — agents must download files before URLs expire
Webhook payloads do not include full submission data in all plans — agents may need to follow up with a GET request to retrieve complete answers
Conditional logic is UI-configured; the API returns all responses regardless of which fields were shown to the respondent
No bulk submission retrieval across multiple forms — must query each form's submissions endpoint separately

security_score_components.security_notes: Single API key with account-wide access and no scope granularity is a risk for agents handling sensitive form data (PII, health info). GDPR-compliant data handling. Webhook payloads should be verified using the provided signature secret to prevent spoofing.
